verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is canonically a secreted serine protease with no transmembrane domain, expressed predominantly in prostate luminal epithelium and secreted into prostatic fluid and serum. It lacks a TM anchor and is not GPI-anchored. However, a 2025 Clinical Cancer Research study (Shen et al.) directly confirmed KLK2 cell-surface expression on prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP) by FACS and confocal microscopy (co-localization with EpCAM), and on freshly dissociated patient mCRPC bone metastases — establishing surface display specifically in the prostate cancer (oncogenic transformation) context. This surface pool is absent/undetected under normal baseline physiology. Multiple clinical-stage surface-directed therapeutics have advanced based on this: pasritamig (KLK2×CD3 bispecific, phase I), JNJ-69086420 (actinium-225-labeled anti-KLK2 RLT), and KLK2-targeted CAR-T. The mechanism of surface retention is not a classical TM anchor but appears linked to the oncogenic state of prostate cancer cells; outside prostate cancer, KLK2 is predominantly a secreted/soluble protease. Verdict: contextual/cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation state enables surface display in prostate cancer cells).
